title: FD Interest Rates 2026 - SBI vs HDFC vs Post Office vs Small Finance Banks
meta_description: FD rates comparison 2026 - SBI, HDFC, ICICI, Post Office, AU Small Finance Bank - who gives highest rate.

hero.one_liner: Compare fixed deposit interest rates across major banks, post office, small finance banks - find best rate for your deposit tenure.

sections.caption: Small finance banks (AU, Equitas) offer highest rates. Post office is best among government-backed options. All bank FDs are DICGC insured up to ₹5 lakh.

sections.heading: Where to Put Your Money
content: Post Office Time Deposit 7.5% for 5 years - government-guaranteed, no DICGC limit. Or SBI/HDFC FD - DICGC covers up to 5L per bank.
Small Finance Banks (AU, Equitas) offer 7.5-8.1% - RBI-regulated, DICGC-insured up to 5L. Keep deposits under 5L per bank for full coverage.
Bajaj Finance and AAA-rated NBFCs offer 8-8.5% - NOT DICGC-insured. Only invest with AAA/AA+ rated companies and amounts you can afford to risk.
Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) at 8.2% beats all FDs. Maximum 30L investment.
After maxing SCSS, use senior citizen FD rates at small finance banks (8.5-8.6%).
Always verify current rates on bank websites before investing.

sections.heading: Strategy: Ladder Your FDs
content: Don't put all money in one FD. Split across tenures and banks for better returns.
10 lakh example: 3L in AU SFB 1-year at 7.75%, 3L in Post Office 5-year at 7.50%, 2L in Bajaj Finance 3-year at 8.25%, 2L in Equitas SFB 5-year at 8.10%.
Benefits: (1) Better average return than single bank, (2) Staggered maturities for liquidity, (3) Diversified risk.

sections.heading: Don't chase the highest rate blindly
sections.content: A 0.5% higher rate on ₹5 lakh for 1 year = ₹2,500 extra interest. If the bank has poor customer service, branch accessibility issues, or you're above the ₹5L DICGC insurance limit, the risk isn't worth ₹2,500. For amounts above ₹5L, stick to SBI/HDFC/ICICI. For amounts under ₹5L, small finance banks are safe and pay more.

faqs.question: Small finance bank FD - is it safe?
faqs.answer: RBI-regulated and DICGC-insured up to 5L. So deposits up to 5L are as safe as SBI. Beyond 5L, slightly more risk. Stick to 5L or less per small finance bank.

faqs.question: Post Office FD vs bank FD - which is safer?
faqs.answer: Post Office backed by Government of India - unlimited guarantee. Bank FD insured by DICGC only up to 5L. For deposits above 5L, Post Office is safer.

faqs.question: Why are small finance bank rates higher?
faqs.answer: They need deposits to grow - offer higher rates as incentive. They lend at higher rates (to MSMEs), so can pay depositors more. RBI-regulated with same compliance as large banks.

faqs.question: Should I break existing FD if rates increase?
faqs.answer: Calculate: penalty (0.5-1% rate reduction) vs gain from new rate. If increase is 0.5%+ and 2+ years remain, breaking often makes sense.

disclaimer: FD rates change periodically. Verify current rates on bank websites before investing.
